description:
Replace walk_spec_for_discovery's per-block terminator scan for the
three callsite slots (Direct, ClosureLit, CallClosureKnown) with an
iteration over Stalled outcomes for the caller fn. For each:
  - Compute the target spec key from caller_ft.block_envs at
    (caller, block).
  - Emit (EmitterSite, target_key) into out.emits.

Cont and MakeClosure slots stay on their existing per-block paths
(per game-walk: they're not 1-to-1 with reducer callsites).

## Acceptance

- Goldens byte-identical.
- Typer LOC drops noticeably.
- All 842 tests green.

## Removes

- walk_spec_for_discovery's per-Term::Call/TailCall/CallClosure/
  TailCallClosure scanning code in the discovery walk.

comments.text: Game-walk note: not every typer-side spec comes from a callsite — some come from SCC widening of existing specs (fib_tailrec emits [int, int, int] and [int, 1, int] this way). Those are NOT callsite outcomes. Keep walk_spec_for_discovery's SCC widening logic; only remove the per-Term::Call/CallClosure scanning.

Investigation findings (2026-05-18, fz-9pr.10 attempt):

The proposed refactor — "replace per-block terminator scan with
iteration over Stalled outcomes" — has a real conceptual benefit
(the outcomes table becomes the source of truth for which callsites
exist) but only a tiny mechanical LOC payoff. The reasons:

1. walk_spec_for_discovery emits PER (caller_spec, block, slot).
   Different specs of the same caller have different block_envs,
   which means different target Descr keys. The outcomes table is
   keyed by CallsiteId (no spec key), so per-walk we still compute
   keys from block_envs.

2. Computing those keys requires terminator-shape access:
   - Direct: callee FnId + arg Vars + n_params padding.
   - CallClosureKnown: closure Var + fn_constants lookup + args.
   - ClosureLit(c, s): closure Var's Descr.funcs[c].pos[s].lit +
     captures + args.
   None of this is encoded in the outcome; the terminator is still
   the source. So we re-walk blocks to access terminators either way.

3. SCC widening (widen_direct) lives in the walk for the same
   reason: it transforms the computed key. It must stay.

4. After fz-9pr.5's invariant fires green, every surviving
   Call/TailCall/CallClosure/TailCallClosure terminator HAS a Stalled
   outcome. Iterating outcomes filtered by caller == iterating
   block-terminators by another name. The set is identical.

What an honest refactor LOOKS like — and why I'm deferring:

The structural shift would be to centralize "given a terminator, what
slot does it produce and what are the parameters" into a small helper,
then drive both the typer's emit path and (later) ir_inline's
inlining decisions off that. That's a more substantial cleanup arc
than this single ticket scopes.

Risks of forcing the wording's exact change:

- The typer's worklist is performance-sensitive and the walk is the
  hot path. Reorganising it without a stronger refactor than
  "iterate outcomes" risks introducing subtle ordering bugs (e.g.,
  the existing code interleaves env building, stmt MakeClosure
  emits, and terminator emits — splitting the terminator emit out
  changes when env is in what state).
- Goldens are byte-identical today; a careless refactor risks
  fixture_matrix or golden_specs drift.

Recommendation: close as superseded-by-investigation OR re-scope as
"extract terminator→slot helper to be shared by reducer / typer /
ir_inline" — which makes more sense before fz-9pr.E (where the
reducer learns inline criterion) than after.

Pausing here; not committing structural changes. fz-9pr.11+ can
proceed without this refactor — the outcome table is already the
unified vocabulary, and the typer reads/writes it (fz-9pr.8). The
per-block scan continues to exist as the computation engine; the
outcomes table is the published record.

Reading post-.17: the per-block scan IS now block_callsites (one site, shared). The ticket's original wording asks to drive emission by iterating Stalled outcomes filtered by caller. After .17 + .8, that translation runs into a slot-vocabulary mismatch:

- Reducer writes Stalled at one canonical slot per terminator: EmitSlot::Direct for Call/TailCall, EmitSlot::ClosureLit(0,0) for CallClosure/TailCallClosure.
- Typer emits at fine-grained slots: Direct, CallClosureKnown, ClosureLit(c,s) per resolved (clause, sig).

So 'iterate Stalled outcomes, build keys' would lose the typer's per-(c,s) ClosureLit emits AND the CallClosureKnown emits (the reducer doesn't write either). Filtering block_callsites output by 'is there a Stalled outcome at this slot' has the same problem.

The .5 invariant ensures every terminator with a surviving call has SOME outcome at the canonical reducer-slot, but the set of typer-emit-slots is a refinement, not an equality.

Two honest paths forward, both out of scope here:
1. Promote reducer's slot vocabulary to match typer's (write CallClosureKnown / ClosureLit(c,s) at reducer-time using fn_constants / Descr from a typer pre-pass). Inverts the dependency.
2. Add a 'canonical slot' projection in CallsiteId and have both sides agree on it.

Closing as superseded-by-.17. The deeper structural unification this ticket pointed at landed under .17 (block_callsites enumerator). Reframe as a follow-up once a wider slot-vocabulary cleanup is on the table.

title: fz-CO.D.3 — Typer reads outcomes; remove per-block scan for Direct/ClosureLit/Known
